# Obsidian fileManager.processFrontMatter — Shim Validation Capture
#
# Producer (intended): .planning/phases/21-v1-2-migration/21-02-PLAN.md Task 4
#   Test 7 — dev-vault shim-capture step. Procedure: open
#   <dev-vault>/leetcode/test-shim-capture.md (synthetic fixture with
#   `lc-slug: shim-capture-test`, no `lc-language`, Python langSlug fence) in
#   live Obsidian; trigger migrateLegacyFenceIfNeeded; capture pre/post text
#   via vault.read; diff post-text against the byte layout produced by the
#   fixture-runner shim's `applyFrontmatterMutation` reconstruction.
#
# Status (Plan 21-02 Task 4 + Plan 21-04 Task 3 inheritance):
#   skipped_capture_date: 2026-06-01
#   obsidian_version: deferred
#   shim_validation: skipped
#   DIFF: deferred
#
# Why skipped: headless executor — no live Obsidian session available during
# the Phase 21 wave-4 worktree run. Plan 21-02 Task 4 Test 7 recorded
# `shim_validation=skipped` in 21-02-SUMMARY.md (line 51, line 181, line 291).
# Plan 21-04 Task 3 inherits the deferral: the `*.expected.md` fixtures and
# the runner's `applyFrontmatterMutation` are both authored against the
# convention "new lc-language is appended at the end of the frontmatter
# block" (the documented convention in 21-04-PLAN.md behavior block). Both
# sides of the equality check use the same reconstruction logic, so the
# fixture suite is internally consistent. Empirical validation against live
# Obsidian remains a follow-up dev-vault session.
#
# Re-run protocol when a dev-vault session becomes available:
#   1. Open the dev vault in Obsidian; create
#      <dev-vault>/leetcode/test-shim-capture.md with frontmatter
#      `lc-slug: shim-capture-test` (NO `lc-language`) and a python3 fence.
#   2. Run the migration command from the command palette (auto path or
#      `LeetCode: Migrate current note`).
#   3. After migration completes, read the post-migration file from the
#      dev-vault filesystem (cat) and compare byte-for-byte against the
#      fixture-runner shim's expected reconstruction (run the shim against
#      the same pre-migration text and diff outputs).
#   4. If diff is empty: replace this file with the captured non-empty
#      output and set `shim_validation: passed` + `DIFF: empty`.
#   5. If diff is non-empty: update `applyFrontmatterMutation` in
#      tests/fixtures/migration/index.test.ts so its reconstruction matches
#      Obsidian's byte layout, then re-run all 10 fixture-runner tests; any
#      *.expected.md that no longer matches must be regenerated from the
#      now-corrected shim. Document the change in this file under DIFF.
#
# Plan 21-04 Task 3's ground-truth check is the fixture-runner test —
# `*.expected.md` files are paired with the shim that produced them, so
# byte-equal assertions remain meaningful even with deferred validation.
# A future dev-vault capture that shows divergence will require regenerating
# all `*.expected.md` from the corrected shim and re-running the suite.
